summary: Restructure concepts/taxonomy.toml to three-tier sectioning: [families.DOMAIN], [families.DOMAIN.FAMILY], [concepts.DOMAIN.FAMILY], and [concept_aliases]. Initial state: families = single-tier mirror (one family per domain, all that domain's concepts inside). Hand-clustering deferred to a later ticket. Per design.md §6.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: TOML restructure to three-tier nesting (concepts.DOMAIN.FAMILY) breaks two existing readers that assume two-level concepts.DOMAIN: guru/retriever.py:_load_taxonomy_labels (runs on every query) and scripts/tag_concepts.py:load_taxonomy. design.md §13 step 2 requires the restructure be a no-op ('the file parses, the sync script works, the tagger prompt renders'). So this ticket also makes both readers nesting-tolerant (recursive leaf-string collection, same concept->definition output as today, NO family enrichment). Family enrichment of load_taxonomy + prompt grouping + PROMPT_VERSION bump remain ticket 17610554's scope. Initial mirror state: one family per domain, family id == domain id (e.g. cosmology.cosmology); §4 two-tier clustering deferred to ea1c2372.

resolution.note: taxonomy.toml restructured to three-tier §6 layout, single-tier mirror state (one family per domain). 88 defs verbatim. Loaders (retriever, tag_concepts) made nesting-tolerant to preserve §13 no-op invariant; family enrichment deferred to 17610554. 9 structure tests + full suite green (231 passed; sole failure is pre-existing unrelated chunking round-trip).
